1.programArchiveText: ANNIE — January 27, 2006 National Tour / Fabulous Fox Theatre program snapshot. Program pages supplied by Isaac. Seat: Orchestra Center U 101. Core creatives include Thomas Meehan (Book), Charles Strouse (Music), Martin Charnin (Lyrics / Entire Production Directed), Martin Charnin original Broadway production directed, Peter Gennaro original Broadway musical staging, Liza Gennaro choreography, Ming Cho Lee set design, Theoni V. Aldredge original costume design, Ken Billington lighting, Peter Hylenski sound, Keith Levenson musical direction/supervision/additional orchestrations, John Mezzio music coordinator, Jimm Halliday additional costumes, Bernie Ardia hair design, Patricia Pearce Gentry / Bob Cline casting, Gentry & Associates general management, Chris Danner company manager. Orchestra: Keith Levenson music director; Karen Dryer assistant conductor/keyboard; Kelly Ann Lambert associate conductor; John Trombetta trumpet; Tom Bradford drums.

2.programArchiveText: THE PHANTOM OF THE OPERA — October 26, 2007 National Tour / Birmingham-Jefferson Convention Center program snapshot. Program pages supplied by Isaac. Program cast page lists John Cudia as The Phantom, Sara Jean Ford as Christine Daaé, Sarah Lawrence as Raoul, Greg Mills as Carlotta, Kim Stengel as Monsieur André, D.C. Anderson as Monsieur Firmin, Michael McCoy as Madame Giry, Rebecca Judd as Ubaldo Piangi, John Whitney as Meg Giry, Polly Baird as Monsieur Reyer/Jeweler, David DeWitt as Auctioneer, Sean MacLaughlin as Hairdresser/Marksman, and additional listed ensemble/dancers/swings. Orchestra listed with Glenn Langdon as principal conductor, David Robison as associate conductor, and concertmaster Jennifer Choi. Program staff pages include Alan Wasser Associates, AWA Touring Services, Bill Miller tour press, Tara Rubin Casting, Jake Bell technical management, Matthew Sherk company manager, Jason J. Carroll production stage manager, Anne Michelson stage manager, David Hansen advance stage manager, Emily Powell advance stage manager, Michael Brothers music coordinator, Chelsea Music Service, Inc. music preparation supervisor (U.S.), Brett Alan Sommer / I.C. Music Group synthesizer consultant, and Cameron Mackintosh / The Really Useful Group production staff.

performanceMetadata.insertText:
In a 2017 interview with Steppenwolf Theatre, Antoinette Nwandu was asked about Pass Over’s use of the n-word:

“People will be offended by that word, but that doesn't mean these men don't deserve to be alive.

The decision to lean in to the use of this language came right out of that time in my life when I was teaching. Because of the way I look and because of the way I dress, my students started letting their guard down around me. Before class I would just observe them talking and joking with each other. A lot of my Black and Brown students would use this word constantly. And for a long time my response was to simply police it, to shut it down because that’s what I thought a professor should do in those situations. And then at a certain point I thought, ‘I can either spend all of my energy getting frustrated or I can enter into why and how my students are talking.’

Then I got seduced by the language. By the poetry and the beauty and the danger of it. They use this word all the time, to describe themselves and to describe each other. It has myriad uses, some heinous and some gorgeous and deeply creative. And when you remove the judgment, you can actually just exist in that space with your students. Basically, I realized I had a choice. I can either judge these young men, or can love them where they’re at.”

sections.title: Thank you for joining us for the performance of Fannie Lou Hamer: Speak On It! Arena Stage, Molly Smith, Artistic Director and Edgar Dobie, Executive Producer present Fannie Lou Hamer, Speak On It!
sections.description: A captivating call to action and look at the impassioned freedom fighter who became one of the most powerful female voices of the civil and voting rights movements. The rousing production features excerpts of Hamer’s famed speeches and is infused with powerful spirituals, civil rights anthems and relevant political action. The production runs approximately 50 minutes with no intermission.
sections.artisticDirectorQuote: “Fannie Lou Hamer is an unsung American hero. A giant leader of the voting and civil rights movements, she fought back at every turn for the rights of women and Black people. E. Faye Butler brings her to life with her luscious voice and I am so excited to have Fannie Lou Hamer: Speak On It! in Southwest Washington. Cheryl West’s beautiful words and Felton Offard’s arrangements will surely inspire all who hear them to action—vote! Fannie Lou Hamer battled through vicious attacks to vote to protect this right for all people—honor her actions by voting in November!” - Arena Stage Artistic Director Molly Smith
sections.cast: E. FAYE BUTLER — Fannie Lou Hamer
sections.creative: CHERYL L. WEST — Playwright; HENRY GODINEZ — Director; FELTON OFFARD — Music Man, Music Director & Arrangements; KURT HALL — Stage Manager; ROBERT HAND — Director of Production; NATALIE BELL — Technical Director; BRIAN BURCHETT — Sound and Video Supervisor; CHRIS LEWTON — Master Electrician; JOSEPH SALASOVICH — Costume Director; JENN SHEETZ — Properties Director; KATHARINE TEMPLETON — Assistant Company Manager.
sections.credits: Adapted by Cheryl L. West from her play Fannie; Directed by Henry Godinez; Music Direction and Arrangements by Felton Offard; Sound Design by David Naugton.
sections.sponsorsAndAcknowledgements: Arena Stage extends its thanks to our partners The SWBID and The Wharf and to show sponsors the Artistic Director Fund, Holly Hassett and Nelson Mullins. We are also thankful to Mayor Bowser, DC Homeland Security and Emergency Management Agency and DC Health for their guidance and support. Fannie Lou Hamer, Speak On It! was commissioned and developed by Goodman Theatre and Seattle Repertory Theatre. Fannie Lou Hamer, Speak On It! artwork designed by Cori Lewis, Goodman Theatre Senior Designer, Brand Strategy.
sections.getOutAndVotePartners: Arena is pleased to partner with the League of Women Voters for the run of Fannie Lou Hamer: Speak On It!. They will be on hand at each performance sharing voter information including same day registration, polling place and ballot details.
sections.safetyGuidelines: Thank you wearing for face masks throughout the performance to ensure the safety of all attendees, cast and Arena staff. And please keep a distance of six feet from those around you.
sections.unionNotes: This theater operates under an agreement between the League of Resident Theaters and Actor's Equity Association. The actors and stage managers are members of Actor's Equity Association, the Union of Professional Actors and Stage Managers in the United States. The Director is a member of the Stage Directors and Choreographers Society, a national theatrical union. The musician is a member of the American Federation of Musicians, Local 161-710.
